description: Deterministic LLM benchmark and league engine for Mafia-style social deduction. v1 implements the mini7_v1 ruleset (2 Mafia, 1 Detective, 1 Doctor, 3 Villagers), an event-sourced replayable engine, a multi-provider LLM adapter (Cerebras zai-glm-4.7 primary, DeepInfra deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash fallback) via LiteLLM, an async tick-barrier game runner, SQLAlchemy 2.x async persistence over SQLite (Postgres-portable), OpenSkill ratings over clone gauntlets, and a FastAPI admin/spectator API. Backend-only — quality gates are ruff + mypy + pytest, no browser verification.

userStories.title: Settings module and structured logging
userStories.description: As an engineer, I need typed configuration loaded from .env and a JSON logger so every later module gets a consistent way to read settings and emit traces.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/settings.py with a `Settings` BaseSettings class (pydantic-settings) that loads CEREBRAS_API_KEY, DEEPINFRA_API_KEY, PADRINO_DB_URL, PADRINO_LOG_LEVEL, PADRINO_LLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, PADRINO_TEMPERATURE, PADRINO_TOP_P, PADRINO_PRIMARY_MODEL, PADRINO_FALLBACK_MODEL from environment with sensible defaults matching .env.example
Settings is exposed via a cached `get_settings()` accessor (functools.lru_cache)
Create src/padrino/logging.py with `configure_logging(level: str)` using structlog → JSON renderer, idempotent so repeated calls are safe
tests/test_settings.py verifies defaults, env-var overrides via monkeypatch, and that get_settings() returns the same instance
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Domain enums and mini7_v1 ruleset constants
userStories.description: As an engineer, I need a single source of truth for factions, roles, role families, phases, action types, and the mini7_v1 numeric constants so every later module references the same vocabulary.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/enums.py with StrEnum classes: Faction (TOWN, MAFIA), Role (MAFIA_GOON, DETECTIVE, DOCTOR, VILLAGER), RoleFamily (DECEPTIVE, INVESTIGATIVE, PROTECTIVE, VANILLA_TOWN), ActionType (NOOP, ABSTAIN, VOTE, MAFIA_KILL, PROTECT, INVESTIGATE), PhaseKind (SETUP, NIGHT_0_MAFIA_INTRO, DAY_DISCUSSION, DAY_VOTE, NIGHT_MAFIA_DISCUSSION, NIGHT_ACTIONS, TERMINAL)

Create src/padrino/core/rulesets/mini7_v1.py exporting: RULESET_ID='mini7_v1', PLAYER_COUNT=7, ROLE_COUNTS={MAFIA_GOON:2, DETECTIVE:1, DOCTOR:1, VILLAGER:3}, DISCUSSION_ROUNDS_PER_DAY=3, MAX_DAYS=5, PUBLIC_MESSAGE_MAX_CHARS=600, PRIVATE_MESSAGE_MAX_CHARS=600, MEMORY_UPDATE_MAX_CHARS=1200, PUBLIC_TRANSCRIPT_RECENT_MESSAGE_LIMIT=80, LLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=45, TEMPERATURE=0.7, TOP_P=1.0
Helper `role_family_for(role: Role) -> RoleFamily` and `faction_for(role: Role) -> Faction` defined and tested
tests/core/test_mini7_constants.py verifies role counts sum to PLAYER_COUNT, all roles have a family, and ruleset is importable as a frozen namespace
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Canonical JSON serializer
userStories.description: As the engine, I need a deterministic JSON encoder so every event hash is reproducible across machines and Python versions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/engine/canonical_json.py exposing `canonical_dumps(obj: Any) -> bytes`
Output is UTF-8 with sorted object keys, no insignificant whitespace, ensure_ascii=False, and rejects float values (raises TypeError) — encode numerics as int or string
Datetimes are rejected (raise TypeError) — caller must pre-serialize timestamps to ISO strings if needed

tests/core/test_canonical_json.py covers: key ordering stable across dict insertion order, nested dicts/lists, unicode strings preserved, float raises, bytes raises, identical inputs produce byte-identical output
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Seeded deterministic RNG
userStories.description: As the engine, I need a pure SHA-256-based RNG so role assignments, reveal orders, and any random choice are reproducible from a seed without using Python's `random` module.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/engine/rng.py with class `SeededRng(seed: str | bytes)` exposing: `next_bytes(n: int) -> bytes`, `randbelow(n: int) -> int` (rejection sampling, never modulo-bias), `shuffle(items: list[T]) -> list[T]` (Fisher-Yates returning new list), `choice(items: Sequence[T]) -> T`
Implementation derives each draw from sha256(seed_bytes + counter.to_bytes(8))
Module-level static assertion / test that `import random` is NOT imported by rng.py

tests/core/test_rng.py verifies: same seed produces same sequence; different seeds produce different sequences; randbelow output strictly in [0, n); shuffle preserves multiset; rng.py does not import `random` (check via ast)
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Hash-chain event hashing function
userStories.description: As the engine, I need a function that derives `event_hash` from `prev_event_hash` plus the canonical-JSON body of an event (excluding the hash field and timestamps) so every game produces a tamper-evident audit log.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/engine/hashing.py with `compute_event_hash(prev_event_hash: str, event_body: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str` returning hex digest
Function strips keys 'event_hash', 'prev_event_hash', and 'created_at' from event_body before hashing
Function uses canonical_dumps from US-003 to ensure deterministic input bytes
`GENESIS_HASH` constant = '0' * 64 is exported and used for the first event in a chain

tests/core/test_hashing.py verifies: same input produces same hash; tampering any non-excluded field changes hash; tampering excluded fields does NOT change hash; chain of three events validates when each prev matches the predecessor
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Game state Pydantic models and phase FSM
userStories.description: As the engine, I need frozen Pydantic state models (Seat, GameState, Phase) and a phase-sequence function so resolvers operate on a typed snapshot.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/engine/state.py with frozen Pydantic models: `Seat` (public_player_id, seat_index, role, faction, alive, death_phase | None, last_protected_target | None, queued_inspection_result | None), `Phase` (kind, day, round), `GameState` (ruleset_id, game_id, game_seed, current_phase, seats, day, terminal_result | None, terminal_reason | None)
Helpers on GameState: `living_seats()`, `living_seats_by_faction(f)`, `seat_by_public_id(id)`, `alive_count_by_faction(f)`

Create src/padrino/core/engine/phases.py with `next_phase(current: Phase, ruleset) -> Phase` implementing the mini7_v1 sequence: SETUP → NIGHT_0_MAFIA_INTRO → DAY_1_DISCUSSION_ROUND_1 → ROUND_2 → ROUND_3 → DAY_1_VOTE → NIGHT_1_MAFIA_DISCUSSION → NIGHT_1_ACTIONS → DAY_2_… up to MAX_DAYS=5
tests/core/test_state.py and tests/core/test_phases.py cover model immutability (mutation raises), helpers, and full phase-sequence enumeration without exceeding MAX_DAYS
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Role assignment from seed
userStories.description: As the engine, I need a deterministic function that turns a game seed into a 7-seat role assignment so every clone-game with the same seed produces the same roster.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/engine/role_assignment.py with `assign_roles(game_seed: str, ruleset) -> list[Seat]` returning 7 seats with public_player_ids P01..P07, seat_index 0..6, and exactly 2 MAFIA_GOON / 1 DETECTIVE / 1 DOCTOR / 3 VILLAGER
Implementation uses SeededRng(role_seed=sha256(b'roles' + game_seed.encode())) and Fisher-Yates over the canonical role multiset
All seats start alive=True, death_phase=None, last_protected_target=None, queued_inspection_result=None

tests/core/test_role_assignment.py verifies: same seed → same assignment; different seeds → different assignments (with overwhelming probability across 100 trials); role counts always exact; public_player_ids unique and 'P01'..'P07'
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Legal action computation per role and phase
userStories.description: As the agent contract layer, I need a pure function that returns the legal action types and legal targets for a given player and phase so observation prompts and response validation share one source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/engine/legal_actions.py with `legal_actions_for(state: GameState, seat: Seat) -> LegalActions` returning a Pydantic model with `allowed_action_types: list[ActionType]` and `legal_targets: list[str]`
Discussion phases: only NOOP, legal_targets=[]
Vote phase: VOTE (targets = living seats other than self) plus ABSTAIN
Mafia discussion phase: only NOOP for mafia, nothing for town (town seats not eligible)

Night actions phase: mafia → MAFIA_KILL targets = living non-mafia; doctor → PROTECT targets = living seats including self EXCEPT last_protected_target; detective → INVESTIGATE targets = living seats other than self; villagers → NOOP only
Dead seats → empty allowed types (caller must skip them)
tests/core/test_legal_actions.py covers every role × every phase × alive/dead and confirms the doctor consecutive-protect constraint
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Day vote resolver
userStories.description: As the engine, I need a pure resolver that takes the set of submitted votes and returns the elimination result so day-vote tests are isolated from runner / event-log concerns.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/engine/resolvers/day_vote.py with `resolve_day_vote(state, submissions: Mapping[str, Action]) -> DayVoteResult` (eliminated: str | None, vote_tally: dict[str, int], reason: str)
Invalid targets (dead, non-existent, self-vote) become ABSTAIN with reason logged in result
Unique plurality of valid votes eliminates that target; ties → eliminated=None; all-abstain → eliminated=None
Submissions for dead players are ignored
tests/core/test_day_vote.py covers: unique-winner; 2-2 tie; all-abstain; self-vote → abstain; vote-for-dead → abstain; missing submission → abstain; chat field never read
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Mafia night kill resolver
userStories.description: As the engine, I need a pure resolver that picks the mafia kill target via unique plurality among living mafia votes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/engine/resolvers/mafia_kill.py with `resolve_mafia_kill(state, mafia_submissions: Mapping[str, Action]) -> MafiaKillResult` (target: str | None, vote_tally: dict[str, int])
Targets must be living non-mafia; invalid targets become no vote
Unique plurality determines target; tie → target=None; no valid votes → target=None
tests/core/test_mafia_kill.py covers: 2-vote agree; 1v1 tie → no kill; mafia-targets-mafia rejected; dead-mafia submission ignored; missing submissions tolerated
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Doctor protect resolver with no-consecutive-repeat
userStories.description: As the engine, I need a pure doctor-protect resolver that enforces the rule that the doctor may not protect the same player two nights in a row.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/engine/resolvers/doctor_protect.py with `resolve_doctor_protect(state, doctor_submission: Action | None) -> DoctorProtectResult` (protected: str | None, reason: str)
If doctor is dead or no submission → protected=None
If submitted target equals doctor's `last_protected_target` → coerced to NOOP, protected=None, reason='REPEAT_VIOLATION'
Self-protect is permitted unless it was the previous-night target
Invalid (non-living) target → NOOP
tests/core/test_doctor_protect.py covers: first-night any target OK; repeat → NOOP; alternating self / other allowed; dead doctor → None; invalid target → None
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Detective investigate resolver
userStories.description: As the engine, I need a pure detective-investigate resolver that produces a MAFIA/TOWN result to be queued for next-day delivery only if the detective survives the night.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/engine/resolvers/detective.py with `resolve_detective_investigation(state, detective_submission: Action | None) -> DetectiveResult` (target: str | None, finding: 'MAFIA' | 'TOWN' | None)
Self-investigation is illegal — coerce to NOOP
Invalid (non-living, non-existent) target → NOOP
Finding is 'MAFIA' if target.faction == MAFIA, else 'TOWN'
Queuing for next-day delivery is the caller's responsibility (this resolver is pure on state)
tests/core/test_detective.py covers: town target → 'TOWN'; mafia target → 'MAFIA'; self → None; dead target → None; no submission → None
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Night resolution composition
userStories.description: As the engine, I need a function that composes mafia-kill, doctor-protect, and detective-investigate results into the actual deaths and queued findings for a night.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/engine/resolvers/night.py with `resolve_night(state, all_submissions) -> NightResolution` (eliminated: str | None, protected: str | None, detective_finding: tuple[str, str] | None, mafia_kill_target: str | None)
If mafia kill target exists and equals protected target → no death (`eliminated=None`)
Else if mafia kill target exists → that seat is eliminated
Detective finding is included only if the detective seat is still alive after eliminations are applied
tests/core/test_night_resolution.py covers: protect saves; protect mismatched → death occurs; no kill, no protect; detective dies same night → finding suppressed; detective lives → finding queued
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Win condition checker
userStories.description: As the engine, I need a pure function that checks Town / Mafia / Draw conditions after every elimination and at MAX_DAYS exhaustion.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/engine/win_conditions.py with `check_win(state) -> WinResult | None` returning ('TOWN', reason) when alive_mafia==0; ('MAFIA', reason) when alive_mafia >= alive_town; ('DRAW', 'MAX_DAYS_REACHED') when day > MAX_DAYS and no prior winner; None otherwise
Function is pure — does not mutate state

tests/core/test_win_conditions.py covers: last mafia eliminated → TOWN; 1-mafia vs 1-town parity → MAFIA; 2-mafia vs 3-town no winner → None; day 5 ends with no winner → DRAW; checking after terminal state is idempotent
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Event type catalog
userStories.description: As the event-sourcing layer, I need a typed catalog of every event the engine can emit so storage and replay both serialize/deserialize through the same discriminated union.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/engine/events.py with a Pydantic discriminated-union `Event` over: GameCreated, RolesAssigned, PhaseStarted, PublicMessageSubmitted, PrivateMessageSubmitted, VoteSubmitted, MafiaKillVoteSubmitted, ProtectSubmitted, InvestigateSubmitted, ActionTimedOut, OutputTruncated, OutputInvalid, DayVoteResolved, NightResolved, DetectiveResultDelivered, PlayerEliminated, PhaseResolved, GameTerminated
Each event carries: event_type (literal), sequence (int), phase (str), visibility ('PUBLIC' | 'PRIVATE' | 'SYSTEM'), actor_player_id (str | None), payload (model-specific fields)
Events are frozen Pydantic models with strict types — no Any payloads
tests/core/test_events.py round-trips every event type through canonical JSON and back
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: In-memory event log writer with hash chain
userStories.description: As the engine, I need an EventLog class that appends events, allocates sequence numbers, and computes hash chains so games produce tamper-evident audit trails before persistence is wired up.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/engine/event_log.py with `EventLog` class: `append(event_body: dict) -> StoredEvent` (assigns sequence, prev_event_hash, event_hash), `head_hash` property, `events` read-only view
Sequence starts at 0 and is strictly contiguous
First event's prev_event_hash == GENESIS_HASH
Class is pure (no DB, no clock) — caller supplies any `created_at` value via the event body, and it is excluded from hashing
tests/core/test_event_log.py covers: contiguous sequences; chain validates; tampering a payload field invalidates downstream hashes; head_hash matches last event
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Reducer dispatching events to state
userStories.description: As the engine, I need a pure reducer `(state, event) -> state` that handles every event type so live execution and replay share the same state-transition logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/engine/reducer.py with `apply_event(state: GameState, event: Event) -> GameState` returning a NEW frozen state (no mutation)

Reducer handles: GameCreated (initializes state), RolesAssigned (sets seats), PhaseStarted (updates current_phase), PlayerEliminated (sets alive=False, death_phase), DetectiveResultDelivered (sets queued_inspection_result), ProtectSubmitted (records last_protected_target for next-night enforcement), GameTerminated (sets terminal_result, terminal_reason)
Message and submission events are recorded but do not mutate mechanical state (chat firewall)
Unknown event types raise; tests confirm exhaustiveness
tests/core/test_reducer.py replays a hand-crafted event sequence and asserts final state matches expected
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Replay modes (engine replay and frozen-response replay)
userStories.description: As the engine, I need deterministic replay entry points: (1) reconstruct final state from a typed event sequence via the reducer; (2) re-seal stored event bodies through a fresh EventLog and prove the hash chain reproduces identically. The full LLM frozen-response replay (engine loop consuming archived AgentResponses) is deferred to a follow-up story once US-021 (AgentResponse) and US-029 (GameRunner / GameConfig) land.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/engine/replay.py with `replay_events(events: Sequence[Event]) -> GameState` that folds the events through `apply_event` starting from `initial_state()`
Create `replay_event_log(stored_events: Sequence[StoredEvent]) -> EventLog` that re-appends each prior event's body to a fresh EventLog and returns it
`replay_event_log` raises `ReplayHashMismatchError` (subclass of ValueError) when a re-derived event_hash disagrees with the prior stored event_hash; the error names the offending sequence number
Both functions are pure (no DB, no clock, no LLM, no network) and respect the pure-core firewall

tests/core/test_replay.py covers: (a) `replay_events` on a hand-crafted 7-seat event stream yields the same final GameState as incremental `apply_event` folding; (b) `replay_event_log` reproduces identical sequence numbers and event_hash values for every prior StoredEvent; (c) tampering with one body in the prior list triggers `ReplayHashMismatchError` at the correct sequence; (d) replay is insensitive to `created_at` differences between original and replayed bodies
tests/core/test_replay.py asserts replay.py imports no DB/runner/LLM/clock modules
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: Originally specified `replay_frozen_responses(GameConfig, Mapping[(phase, seat), AgentResponse])`, but GameConfig (US-029) and AgentResponse (US-021) do not yet exist. Rescoped to the two deterministic replay primitives that depend only on already-shipped modules (events, reducer, event_log, hashing). The LLM frozen-response replay will be tracked as a follow-up story when its dependencies are in place.

userStories.title: Observation builder
userStories.description: As the agent contract layer, I need a function that builds the JSON observation given to a seat for a phase, including public events, private events, and legal actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/observations.py with `build_observation(state, seat, event_log, ruleset) -> Observation` (Pydantic model)

Observation fields match prd.md §6.1: ruleset_id, game_public_id, phase, day, round, you (player_id/alive/role/faction), alive_players, dead_players (with cause), public_events (most recent 80), private_events (role-appropriate), legal_actions, your_private_memory placeholder, message_limits
Mafia seats additionally get mafia_teammates and mafia-channel events
Detective seats additionally get prior eligible inspection results
Doctor seats additionally get previous-night protected target (to enforce no-repeat client-side)
tests/core/test_observation_builder.py verifies field presence for each role × phase combo and that town never sees mafia teammates or private mafia chat
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Observation privacy filter for ranked mode
userStories.description: As the benchmark, I need a guard that strips identity, ratings, and meta-game information from ranked observations so no agent gains a side-channel advantage.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/observation_privacy.py with `assert_ranked_observation_safe(obs: Observation) -> None` raising if any forbidden field appears

Forbidden in ranked mode (asserted): any mention of agent_build_id, model_id, provider name, ratings, win rates, gauntlet clone index, other games' transcripts, hidden roles (other than the agent's own role and mafia teammates for mafia)
Function is invoked by the runner before dispatching to the LLM adapter in ranked mode
tests/core/test_observation_privacy.py constructs an observation with each forbidden field present and asserts the function raises; clean observations pass
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Agent response schema and strict JSON validation
userStories.description: As the agent contract layer, I need a Pydantic schema for agent responses that enforces field presence, types, and action consistency before any mechanical resolution.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/agents/contract.py with `AgentResponse` Pydantic model: public_message (str | None), private_message (str | None), action (Action with type and target), memory_update (str), rationale_summary (str | None)
`parse_agent_response(raw_text: str) -> AgentResponse | ResponseError` returns a tagged result — never raises
Invalid JSON → ResponseError(reason='INVALID_JSON')
Schema violations → ResponseError(reason='SCHEMA_VIOLATION', details=...)
Over-limit messages are NOT truncated here — that is the sanitizer's job (US-022)
tests/agents/test_contract.py covers valid response, malformed JSON, missing fields, wrong types, extra unknown fields
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Output sanitizer
userStories.description: As the engine, I need deterministic surface normalization on visible chat so cosmetic encoding tricks cannot fingerprint or steganographically signal across messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/agents/sanitizer.py with `sanitize_visible_text(raw: str, max_chars: int) -> SanitizationResult` (cleaned: str, truncated: bool, replacements: list[str])

Steps in order: Unicode NFKC normalize → strip zero-width and control characters (except newline) → collapse whitespace runs to single space → limit repeated punctuation to 3 identical → strip markdown code blocks and tables → replace URLs with '[URL]' → replace long base64/hex-like strings (>24 chars) with '[ENCODED]' → enforce max length (truncate suffix)
Both raw and sanitized text are returned so the LLM call archive stores both
tests/agents/test_sanitizer.py covers each transformation in isolation and chained, plus the truncation flag
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Schema-failure coercion to NOOP/ABSTAIN
userStories.description: As the runner, I need a function that maps any agent-response failure or timeout to a deterministic engine-safe action so a broken model can never crash a game.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/core/agents/coercion.py with `coerce_to_safe_action(phase: Phase, error_reason: str) -> Action` returning ABSTAIN in vote phases and NOOP everywhere else

Create `coerce_response_failure(phase: Phase, error_reason: str) -> AgentResponse` returning a response with public_message=None, private_message=None, safe action, memory_update='', rationale_summary=None
tests/agents/test_coercion.py covers vote phase → ABSTAIN; night actions → NOOP; discussion → NOOP; same regardless of error_reason
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Async tick barrier with hard timeout
userStories.description: As the runner, I need a coroutine that builds observations for every eligible seat, dispatches them to the LLM adapter concurrently, and returns the validated responses once every call has either completed or timed out — never earlier.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/runner/tick.py with `async def run_tick(state, event_log, eligible_seats, adapter, timeout_s, ruleset) -> dict[str, AgentResponse]`
Uses `asyncio.gather` with per-call `asyncio.wait_for(timeout_s)` so a slow agent cannot block faster agents from running but the gather waits for all
On timeout: ActionTimedOut event recorded, response coerced via US-023
On parse/schema failure: OutputInvalid event recorded, response coerced via US-023
Observation privacy filter (US-020) is invoked in ranked mode before adapter dispatch
tests/runner/test_tick.py uses a mock adapter that mixes slow/fast/error responses and confirms: no early return; all events recorded; coerced actions present for failures
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: Also created src/padrino/llm/adapter.py (LlmAdapter Protocol + AdapterResult) ahead of US-025 since tick.py depends on these types. US-025 will add the DeterministicMockAdapter and may extend the Protocol signature with agent_build.

userStories.title: Scripted agent and deterministic mock LLM adapter
userStories.description: As the test suite, I need a deterministic mock adapter that returns canned responses keyed by (phase_id, public_player_id) and a scripted-agent factory so integration tests can drive full games without any network or randomness.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/llm/adapter.py defining `LlmAdapter` protocol with `async def complete(observation: Observation, agent_build) -> AdapterResult` (raw_response, parsed_response, latency_ms, input_tokens, output_tokens, cost_usd, provider_response_id, status, error)
Create src/padrino/llm/mock.py with `DeterministicMockAdapter(script: Mapping[tuple[str, str], AgentResponse])` returning the scripted response for each (phase_id, player_id) key
Mock adapter records each invocation in a public `.calls` list for test assertions
Create `make_villager_script`, `make_mafia_script`, `make_town_win_script`, `make_mafia_win_script` helpers in tests/conftest.py
tests/llm/test_mock_adapter.py verifies determinism and key-miss raises KeyError
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: LlmAdapter.complete signature kept as (observation) — agent_build is bound at adapter construction time (per US-024). Conftest helpers are parametric builders; the make_*_win_script scripts assume the canonical (P01,P02 mafia; P03..P07 town) seat layout that will be wired up by US-027.

userStories.title: Game runner orchestration loop
userStories.description: As the runner, I need a `GameRunner` that loops phases — build observations, run tick, resolve, write events, check win — until the game terminates.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/runner/game_runner.py with `async def run_game(config: GameConfig, adapter: LlmAdapter, ranked: bool) -> GameOutcome` (final_state, event_log, llm_calls)
Loop order per phase: phase_started event → eligible seats → run_tick → record submission events → resolver → resolution events → win check → next_phase
Terminates on win/draw OR on day > MAX_DAYS
Each phase yields control via `await asyncio.sleep(0)` so the event loop stays responsive
Reducer is applied after every event so the in-memory state stays consistent
tests/runner/test_game_runner.py runs a short scripted game and confirms terminal state + event-log hash chain integrity
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: End-to-end scripted-game integration tests
userStories.description: As the test suite, I need three end-to-end scripted games (Town win, Mafia win, Draw at MAX_DAYS) that exercise the runner from start to terminal state via the mock adapter.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/integration/test_scripted_games.py implements three test functions, each constructing a full DeterministicMockAdapter script for all 7 seats across all phases of an entire game
Town-win test: scripted votes/actions eliminate both mafia by Day 3
Mafia-win test: scripted votes/protect/no-detect cause mafia to reach parity by Day 4
Draw test: scripted abstain-heavy play reaches Day > MAX_DAYS with no winner
Each test asserts: terminal_result matches expected; alive_count never increases; no events emitted after GameTerminated; event log hash-chain validates end-to-end
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Hypothesis stateful invariant tests
userStories.description: As the test suite, I need property-based stateful tests that randomly drive the engine and confirm invariants hold across many generated traces.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/core/test_invariants.py uses Hypothesis `RuleBasedStateMachine` to generate legal/illegal action sequences

Invariants asserted: alive_count is monotone non-increasing; a player dies at most once; dead players never produce visible messages; role counts never change; terminal game has exactly one terminal result; replay of generated event log equals live state
Use `max_examples=50` and `stateful_step_count=80` to keep CI fast
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: SQLAlchemy async models for core schema
userStories.description: As the persistence layer, I need SQLAlchemy 2.x async ORM models for model_providers, model_configs, prompt_versions, agent_builds, leagues, gauntlets, gauntlet_roster_slots, games, game_seats.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/db/base.py with `Base = DeclarativeBase` and async session factory
Create src/padrino/db/models.py with the nine table models matching prd.md §12 (excluding game_events, llm_calls, ratings, rating_events — those come in later stories)
All UUIDs use `Mapped[uuid.UUID]` with `default=uuid.uuid4`
All timestamps use `Mapped[datetime]` with timezone=True
JSON columns use `JSON` type (SQLite-compatible; Postgres-portable)
tests/db/test_models.py instantiates each model in an in-memory aiosqlite DB and asserts FK constraints fire on invalid inserts
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

src/padrino/db/migrations/env.py wired to async engine + Base.metadata
src/padrino/db/migrations/versions/0001_initial.py creates all tables from US-029
`uv run alembic upgrade head` against a fresh SQLite file succeeds
tests/db/test_migrations.py runs upgrade head and downgrade base against in-memory aiosqlite

userStories.title: Async repository layer for builds/leagues/gauntlets/games
userStories.description: As the API layer, I need async repository classes that encapsulate CRUD for the nine tables from US-029 so routes never touch SQLAlchemy directly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/db/repositories/ with one module per entity: providers.py, model_configs.py, prompt_versions.py, agent_builds.py, leagues.py, gauntlets.py, games.py
Each repository exposes `async def create(session, …) -> Model`, `async def get(session, id) -> Model | None`, `async def list(session, filters) -> list[Model]`
gauntlet repository includes `async def add_roster_slot(session, gauntlet_id, slot_index, agent_build_id)`
games repository includes `async def list_by_gauntlet(session, gauntlet_id)`
tests/db/test_repositories.py runs end-to-end CRUD against an in-memory aiosqlite DB for each repository

userStories.title: Event and llm_call persistence wired to runner
userStories.description: As the runner, I need to persist each event and each LLM call to the DB as the game runs so transcripts and replays survive a process restart.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend src/padrino/db/models.py with `GameEvent` and `LlmCall` models matching prd.md §12
Add alembic migration 0002 creating those two tables
Create src/padrino/db/repositories/events.py with `append_event(session, game_id, event_body) -> GameEvent` and `list_events(session, game_id, visibility_filter)`
Create src/padrino/db/repositories/llm_calls.py with `record_call(session, …)` capturing request_json, raw_response, parsed_response, latency_ms, tokens, cost, provider_response_id, status, error
Wire GameRunner (US-026) to call these repositories after every event/LLM call
tests/db/test_event_persistence.py runs a short scripted game with a real aiosqlite DB and asserts the persisted event chain matches the in-memory event log

uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Provider-agnostic LLM adapter interface
userStories.description: As the LLM layer, I need a provider-agnostic `LlmAdapter` interface so the runner is decoupled from any specific SDK. The DeterministicMockAdapter (US-025) already conforms; this story finalizes the contract and the abstract base.
acceptanceCriteria: Promote `LlmAdapter` from US-025 to a `typing.Protocol` in src/padrino/llm/adapter.py with explicit method signatures
Add `RoutingPolicy` Pydantic model: primary_model: str, fallback_model: str | None, max_retries: int, retry_on_error_classes: list[str]
Add `AgentBuild` Pydantic value object (provider, model_id, prompt_version, inference_params, adapter_version) decoupled from the SQLAlchemy model
tests/llm/test_adapter_protocol.py confirms DeterministicMockAdapter satisfies the Protocol (runtime_checkable assertion)
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: LiteLLM-backed adapter with Cerebras primary and DeepInfra fallback
userStories.description: As the LLM layer, I need a real LiteLLM-backed adapter that routes calls to cerebras/zai-glm-4.7 by default and falls back to deepinfra/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash on failure.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/llm/litellm_adapter.py implementing the `LlmAdapter` protocol via `litellm.acompletion`
Adapter accepts RoutingPolicy and AgentBuild; calls primary model first; on non-retryable error or timeout calls fallback exactly once
Both attempts are recorded as separate LlmCall rows (status='OK', 'FALLBACK_OK', 'PRIMARY_FAILED', or 'BOTH_FAILED')
Latency, input_tokens, output_tokens, and cost_usd are extracted from the LiteLLM response when present
Response body is parsed via AgentResponse contract (US-021); failures coerced via US-023

tests/llm/test_litellm_adapter.py uses unittest.mock to stub litellm.acompletion and verifies: success path; primary failure → fallback success; both failures → coerced safe response with status='BOTH_FAILED'
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Real-provider integration test for one game day
userStories.description: As the test suite, I need an integration test that runs a single mini7_v1 game day against real Cerebras + DeepInfra providers, skipped if API keys are absent.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/integration/test_real_providers.py is marked `@pytest.mark.integration`
Test uses `pytest.skip` if CEREBRAS_API_KEY is unset
Test runs Day 1 only (one discussion round + vote + night) of a mini7_v1 game using all 7 seats backed by the LiteLLM adapter
Asserts: every LLM call has a non-empty raw_response OR was archived with status indicating fallback / failure; event hash chain validates; no exceptions escape
Cost cap: test fails if total cost_usd exceeds $0.10 across all calls (sanity guardrail)
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes (this test does NOT run in default suite)

userStories.title: Gauntlet scheduler with deterministic seed derivation
userStories.description: As the gauntlet layer, I need a scheduler that takes a gauntlet config and produces N child games with deterministically-derived seeds.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/gauntlets/scheduler.py with `async def create_gauntlet(session, league_id, ruleset_id, prompt_version_id, clone_count, gauntlet_seed, roster) -> GauntletCreated` (gauntlet_id, game_ids)
Validates roster length matches PLAYER_COUNT=7 (NOT 15)
Validates clone_count in [1, 100]
Derives per-game seeds: game_seed_i = sha256_hex(b'game' + gauntlet_seed.encode() + i.to_bytes(4))
Inserts the gauntlet row, roster slots, and N game rows in one transaction
tests/gauntlets/test_scheduler.py covers happy path, roster-length-mismatch rejection, clone-count bounds, and seed determinism
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: OpenSkill rating service (global and faction scopes)
userStories.description: As the rating layer, I need a service that wraps OpenSkill PlackettLuce to update agent-build ratings for global and faction scopes after each game.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/ratings/openskill_service.py with `update_ratings_for_game(session, league_id, game_result, agent_builds_by_seat) -> list[RatingEvent]`
Updates two scopes per agent_build: scope_type='GLOBAL' / scope_value='global' and scope_type='FACTION' / scope_value in {'TOWN','MAFIA'}
Uses OpenSkill's `PlackettLuce` model with initial mu=25.0, sigma=25/3
Town team = list of seats where faction==TOWN; Mafia team = MAFIA
Town win → ranks [Town=1, Mafia=2]; Mafia win → [Mafia=1, Town=2]; Draw → equal ranks
conservative_score = mu - 3*sigma persisted on every update
tests/ratings/test_openskill_service.py covers winner mu↑, loser mu↓, sigma↓ after rated game, draw equal-rank, role-family scope NOT updated in v1
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Rating updates wired to game completion
userStories.description: As the runner, I need to call the rating service whenever a game terminates in a ranked gauntlet so leaderboard data accumulates automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend GameRunner (US-026) to invoke `update_ratings_for_game` after the GameTerminated event when ranked=True
Rating updates are persisted in the SAME transaction as the GameTerminated event so partial failures roll back
Each rating update emits a RatingEvent audit row with before/after mu and sigma
tests/runner/test_rating_integration.py runs a scripted Town-win game and asserts both Town seats' ratings updated upward and Mafia seats' ratings updated downward
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Gauntlet completion and provisional flag logic
userStories.description: As the gauntlet layer, I need a function that aggregates child-game outcomes, marks the gauntlet COMPLETED, and computes the provisional flag for each agent build's leaderboard entry.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/padrino/gauntlets/completion.py with `async def finalize_gauntlet_if_done(session, gauntlet_id) -> GauntletFinalized | None`
Marks gauntlet status='COMPLETED' once all child games are terminal
Provisional flag per agent_build: provisional=True if total_games < 30 OR mafia_games < 5 OR town_games < 15
Returns aggregate diagnostics: games_completed, timeout_rate, invalid_action_rate, average_public_message_chars
tests/gauntlets/test_completion.py covers all-games-terminal → COMPLETED, mid-progress → returns None, provisional threshold computation

uv run mypy src tests passes

userStories.title: League and gauntlet routes with roster validation
userStories.description: As the API layer, I need POST /leagues and POST /gauntlets routes that validate roster size = 7 and trigger gauntlet scheduling.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /leagues accepts {name, ruleset_id, ranked} and returns the created league
POST /gauntlets accepts {league_id, ruleset_id, prompt_version_id, clone_count, gauntlet_seed?, roster[]} and returns {gauntlet_id, status:'QUEUED', game_ids[]}
Validation rejects: roster length != 7 (HTTP 422), unknown agent_build_id, clone_count out of [1,100], incompatible prompt/ruleset
If gauntlet_seed omitted, server generates a 256-bit random hex (using os.urandom; runner core stays pure)
GET /gauntlets/{id} returns status, child games, and aggregate diagnostics from US-040
tests/api/test_gauntlet_routes.py covers happy path and each rejection
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Game inspection routes (get, events, transcript, replay)
userStories.description: As the API layer, I need GET /games/{id}, GET /games/{id}/events, GET /games/{id}/transcript, and POST /games/{id}/replay so spectators (and later a frontend) can inspect game data.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /games/{id} returns public game summary (status, terminal_result, current_phase, seat count)
GET /games/{id}/events?visibility=public returns the filtered event log; private events are returned only if the request carries an admin token (header X-Padrino-Admin-Token matches settings)
GET /games/{id}/transcript returns the post-game artifact: public chat, mafia chat, roles (revealed after terminal), actions, outcome
POST /games/{id}/replay invokes the engine-replay path and returns {game_id, replay_status:'PASS'|'FAIL', final_event_hash}
tests/api/test_game_routes.py covers each route, public/private visibility filtering, and replay PASS
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Leaderboard route with provisional flag
userStories.description: As the API layer, I need GET /leagues/{id}/leaderboard returning OpenSkill global + faction scores per agent build with a provisional flag.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /leagues/{id}/leaderboard returns the JSON contract from prd.md §10.4: leaderboard_id, ruleset_id, prompt_version, rating_model, entries[]
Each entry includes: agent_build_id, display_name, games, wins, draws, losses, mu, sigma, conservative_score, timeout_rate, invalid_action_rate, public_message_avg_chars, provisional
role_family_breakdown field is present but empty {} in v1 (placeholder for v1.1)
Sorted by conservative_score desc
tests/api/test_leaderboard.py constructs a small league with 2 agent builds, runs 3 scripted games, and asserts ordering + provisional flag values

userStories.title: Demo gauntlet CLI command and README quickstart
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a `padrino demo-gauntlet` CLI command that creates a 5-clone gauntlet with the mock adapter (or real Cerebras if --real flag) so a fresh checkout can produce a working demo.
acceptanceCriteria: `padrino demo-gauntlet --seed SEED [--real] [--clones N]` creates an in-memory or file-backed gauntlet, runs all child games, prints the final leaderboard
Default is `--clones 5`, mock adapter (no API key needed). `--real` switches to LiteLLM adapter with Cerebras+DeepInfra
Command writes SQLite to ./padrino-demo.db by default
README quickstart section updated with the exact command and expected output preview
tests/cli/test_demo_gauntlet.py runs the command in mock mode against an in-memory DB and asserts a leaderboard JSON is printed
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Coverage gate and core engine purity check
userStories.description: As the project, I need CI to enforce ≥85% coverage on the core engine and verify no impure imports leak in.
acceptanceCriteria: Update CI workflow to fail if coverage on src/padrino/core/** drops below 85%

Add tests/core/test_purity.py that walks src/padrino/core/ with `ast` and asserts no module imports: padrino.db, padrino.llm, padrino.api, padrino.runner, sqlalchemy, litellm, httpx, random, secrets, asyncio, datetime (datetime.timezone is OK if only used for type hints — flag with comment if needed)
Add tests/core/test_no_wallclock.py that asserts no `datetime.utcnow`, `datetime.now`, `time.time` call sites under src/padrino/core/
Update README quickstart with the coverage command
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Observability hooks across gauntlet → game → phase → llm_call
userStories.description: As an operator, I need structured-log traces that connect a gauntlet to its games, each game to its phases, each phase to its LLM calls and events so diagnostics are tractable.
acceptanceCriteria: Use structlog contextvars to bind gauntlet_id, game_id, phase_id, llm_call_id at the right levels in the runner / gauntlet scheduler / LiteLLM adapter

Emit structured events: game.started, phase.started, llm.call.started, llm.call.completed, llm.call.timeout, phase.resolved, game.completed, rating.updated — each at structlog INFO with the correlated IDs

Add a metrics-summary helper that aggregates: games completed, average phase duration, LLM latency p50/p95 per model, timeout rate, invalid JSON rate — exposed via `padrino metrics` CLI subcommand reading from the DB
tests/observability/test_logging_context.py captures structlog output and asserts the correlation IDs propagate
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
